trades.sentiment_summary: First Majestic Silver Corp (AG) reported a profitable 2025 with $211M net income and Q4 revenue of $463.9M, up 169% year-over-year, driving shares to an all-time high of $27.55 and a 65% YTD gain amid strong silver prices and production.[1] Retail sentiment on Stocktwits shifted to 'bullish' with high message volumes, and the stock has surged over 413% in the last 12 months, with speculation of reaching $30.[1] No other significant AG-related news from the last 24 hours was identified.

Analysts from Evercore ISI and Barclays express optimism on Dell Technologies ahead of its Q4 FY2026 earnings on February 26, citing strong AI server demand with $12.3 billion in Q3 orders and a $18.4 billion backlog, expecting revenue and EPS to exceed consensus of $31.4-31.5 billion and $3.52-3.53.[1][2][7] While Wells Fargo and Evercore lowered price targets to $180 and $160 respectively, they maintain Buy/Outperform ratings with significant upside potential.[1][2] Challenges include PC market competition, supply-chain costs, and a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell), but AI growth remains a key positive driver.[4]

Becton Dickinson (BDX) announced early tender results for its upsized $2B debt tender offers on February 25, 2026, following the initial launch that triggered a 17.22% stock drop and $12.29B market cap loss. An EVP sold shares worth $334,005 under a pre-planned Rule 10b5-1 transaction. Positive developments include FDA clearance for a breast biopsy system, a $110M syringe production expansion, a Stifel price target raise to $215 (Buy), and a new urine testing kit launch.

Albertsons Companies reported solid Q3 fiscal 2025 results with net sales rising 1.9% to $19.12 billion and identical sales growth of 2.4%, supported by 21% digital sales growth[6]. However, analyst sentiment remains mixed, with a consensus 'Hold' rating and recent analyst downgrades of price targets from $24-25 to $23, though the company announced a $0.15 per share dividend and completed a $2.1 billion senior notes offering[5][6].

B. Riley Securities raised its price target for CENX to $68 from $64 while reaffirming a Buy rating on February 24, 2026, amid a pattern of upward analyst revisions[1]. The stock hit a 52-week high of $55.75 recently, with strong momentum including shares up 14.34% in the past week and positive earnings estimate revisions[2][4]. Analysts highlight supply tightness boosting profitability, a faster-than-expected smelter restart in Iceland, and elevated EBITDA run-rate as key positives[3].

AES announced a major deal with Google for 20-year power purchase agreements to supply energy for a new data center in Texas, causing the stock to rise 2% on Tuesday. Analysts highlight AES as undervalued with a low forward P/E, strong Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), and positive Earnings ESP ahead of Q4 2025 earnings on February 26, predicting a beat. The company benefits from data center demand, renewable investments, and superior returns compared to industry peers.

DuPont de Nemours (DD) received positive analyst updates, including Citigroup boosting its position by 20.2% in Q3 and price target increases from Deutsche Bank ($46 to $58, Buy) and Mizuho ($46 to $52, Outperform), contributing to a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The company launched the FilmTec™ MXP RO-8038-FF reverse osmosis element on February 4, 2026, targeting dairy processors for improved throughput. Recent stock price data shows a climb to $50.41 on February 20 before declining to $50.08 on February 23, amid strong momentum scores and outperforming market returns.

Axalta reported record full-year 2025 results, including adjusted EBITDA of $1,128 million at a 22.0% margin, record operating cash flow of $649 million, and free cash flow of $466 million, alongside Q4 net sales of $1,262 million and adjusted EBITDA of $272 million[1][2]. The company announced an all-stock merger of equals with AkzoNobel, targeting $600 million in cost synergies, 100-200 bps revenue uplift, and closure in late 2026 or early 2027[1][5]. Analyst consensus remains a Buy rating from 14 analysts as of February 25, 2026[3].

ADMA Biologics is set to report its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results after market close on February 25, 2026, with analysts estimating EPS of $0.20 amid expectations of meeting or exceeding prior revenue guidance of $510-511M for 2025.[1][2][3] Recent announcements have elicited mild positive market reactions, including a 0.25% stock gain, building on strong prior quarters with revenue growth, net income beats, and raised multi-year targets up to $635M for 2026.[2][6] Coverage highlights investor anticipation for positive guidance updates, though some note risks like plasma supply constraints.[3]

Credo Technology (CRDO) announced a partnership with TensorWave on February 25, 2026, to deploy ZeroFlap cables and optics in AMD-based AI clusters, promising up to 1000x reliability, though the stock slipped slightly despite the positive AI infrastructure news[6][7]. Wall Street anticipates strong earnings growth for the quarter ended January 2026, with expected EPS of $0.96 (+284% YoY) and revenue of $389.43M (+188.5% YoY), ahead of the March 2 report[5]. Recent preliminary Q3 FY2026 results showed revenue beating estimates at $404-408M, driving significant stock gains[3][4].

Brown & Brown announced a 10% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.165 per share and expanded its share repurchase program by $1.25 billion, signaling strong confidence in future performance. New February 2026 options trading highlighted attractive covered call strategies with potential 4.90% returns. Recent insider transactions involved routine tax-withholding share dispositions, while stock has declined 5.8% since the last earnings report.

BMNR stock jumped in premarket trading on Wednesday as Ethereum staged a recovery after shedding roughly 38% over the past month[5]. B. Riley Financial cut its price target from $47 to $30 on February 23, citing valuation challenges from $8 billion in unrealized ETH losses, though the company continues pivoting toward fee-generating staking operations with the MAVAN launch targeted for early 2026[2]. Technical analysis suggests a bullish flag pattern with potential targets at $25 and $30, supported by strong institutional backing from ARK Invest, Pantera Capital, and over 400 institutional investors holding nearly 50% of shares[4].

No significant news articles about Amkor Technology (AMKR) were published in the last 24 hours (since February 24, 2026, 4 PM UTC). The most recent items include stock price movements on February 24 showing shares trading between $47.36 and $49.01, and earlier analyst upgrades from Needham, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs following Q4 2025 earnings beats reported on February 9. A large insider sale by 915 Investments on February 12 amounted to $484.9 million, amid recent stock volatility.

BOSS Zhipin (BZ) continues aggressive share repurchases, having deployed over RMB340 million in 2026 under its USD250 million authorized program active through August 2026.[1] The stock traded near $17.21-$17.48 with analysts maintaining a 'Moderate Buy' consensus and an average price target of $24.00, though shares remain below target levels.[2][3]

Comcast maintained its $1.32 annualized dividend for 2026 and reported strong operational metrics including 1.5M wireless net additions and 44M Peacock subscribers (+22% YoY), though adjusted EBITDA declined 10.3% in 4Q25. The company is executing rural fiber expansion and quantum routing initiatives, but faces headwinds from intensifying broadband competition and rising content costs.

No specific news articles from the last 24 hours directly mention AS stock (likely Amer Sports, Inc.). Broader market coverage highlights positive sentiment in tech and S&P 500 driven by Trump comments, Nvidia optimism ahead of earnings, and buy ratings on Amazon and Nvidia. Energy stock NRG saw gains on strong earnings, but no AS-specific updates appear in recent results.

Match Group announced plans to release Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings on February 3, 2026, with the stock gaining 1.33% on February 24 amid technical buy signals and a positive short-term forecast.[1][2] Board members Pamela Seymon and former CEO Sharmistha Dubey will depart at the 2026 Annual Meeting, raising questions about future oversight and strategy.[6] Recent Q4 2025 earnings beat estimates with EPS of $1.06 and revenue of $878M, though shares have underperformed YTD.[3]
